(b) Write down the mathematical names for two different quadrilaterals with
\begin{parts}

(c) two lines of symmetry

The diagram shows a quadrilateral on a 1 cm$^2$ grid.

Find the area of this quadrilateral.

The diagram shows a quadrilateral $DEFG$ and a straight line $FGH$.

(i) Angle $DEF = 82^\circ$.

Write down the mathematical name for this type of angle.

(ii) Work out the value of $x$.

Give a geometrical reason for your answer.

because

because

(a) \begin{tabular}{|c|}
    \hline
    **Garage A** \\
    \$1.41 per litre \\
    \hline
\end{tabular}

\begin{tabular}{|c|}
    \hline
    **Garage B** \\
    \$1.50 per litre \\
    \hline
\end{tabular}
(i) (b) Tiya buys 55 litres of fuel from garage A.

Work out the change she receives from \$100.

(c) Work out how much cheaper it is to buy 20 litres of fuel from garage A than from garage B.

(d) These are the amounts that 6 people spend on fuel at garage A.
\$63  \$84.50  \$72.23  \$46  \$54.10  \$80
Calculate the mean number of litres that they buy.

(e) The cost of fuel at garage B increases from \$1.50 to \$1.53.

Calculate the percentage increase.

(f) The fuel tank of a car is $\frac{2}{5}$ full.

It takes 39 more litres of fuel to fill the tank.

Work out the number of litres of fuel in a full tank.

(g) (i) (h) Use 1 litre = 0.22 gallons to complete this conversion graph.

(i) Use 1 litre = 0.22 gallons to complete this statement.

1 gallon =  litres.

(j) A cylindrical tank for storing fuel has radius 1.5 metres and height 8 metres.

Calculate the volume of the tank in litres.
texHtml:
(a)
**Garage A**
\$1.41 per litre
**Garage B**
\$1.50 per litre
(i) (b) Tiya buys 55 litres of fuel from garage A. Work out the change she receives from \$100. (c) Work out how much cheaper it is to buy 20 litres of fuel from garage A than from garage B. (d) These are the amounts that 6 people spend on fuel at garage A. \$63 \$84.50 \$72.23 \$46 \$54.10 \$80 Calculate the mean number of litres that they buy. (e) The cost of fuel at garage B increases from \$1.50 to \$1.53. Calculate the percentage increase. (f) The fuel tank of a car is $\frac{2}{5}$ full. It takes 39 more litres of fuel to fill the tank. Work out the number of litres of fuel in a full tank. (g) (i) (h) Use 1 litre = 0.22 gallons to complete this conversion graph. (i) Use 1 litre = 0.22 gallons to complete this statement. 1 gallon = litres. (j) A cylindrical tank for storing fuel has radius 1.5 metres and height 8 metres. Calculate the volume of the tank in litres.

tex:
(a) In triangle $DEF$, $DE = 6$ cm and $DF = 4.8$ cm.

Using a ruler and compasses only, construct triangle $DEF$.

Leave in your construction arcs.

The line $EF$ has been drawn for you.

(b) 
(i) (c) Write down the letter of the triangle that is congruent to triangle $T$.

(d) Write down the letter of the triangle that is similar but not congruent to triangle $T$.

(e) The diagram shows an isosceles triangle.

(i) (f) Show that the perpendicular height, $h$, is 6.58 cm, correct to 3 significant figures.

(g) Calculate the area of the triangle.

Give the units of your answer.

(h) Kalpit tries to arrange some of these triangles to make a regular polygon with centre $O$.

Show that Kalpit cannot make a regular polygon.

(a) A shop sells 58 televisions in one week.

The bar chart shows the number of televisions that the shop sells on five of the days.

(i) (b) Write down the number of televisions that the shop sells on Monday.

(c) Find the fraction of the televisions that the shop sells on Sunday.

(d) The number of televisions that the shop sells on the other two days is in the ratio

Wednesday : Friday = 2 : 3.

Complete the bar chart.

(e) Write down the mode.

(f) A television has a price of \$550.

This price is reduced by 4\%.

Calculate the new price of this television.

(g) The scatter diagram shows the prices of different sized televisions.

Write down the type of correlation shown in the scatter diagram.

(h) Hemang buys two televisions.

The probability that a television is faulty is 0.02.

(i) (i) Complete the tree diagram.

(j) Find the probability that Hemang buys two faulty televisions.

(k) The shop sells 4150 televisions in one year.

Calculate the expected number of faulty televisions.

(a) (i) (b) Complete the table of values for $y = -x^2 + 5x + 7$.
\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
$x$ & -1 & 0 & 1 & 2 & 3 & 4 & 5 & 6 \\
\hline
$y$ & 1 & 7 & 11 &  &  & 11 & 7 & 1 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

(c) On the grid, draw the graph of $y = -x^2 + 5x + 7$ for $-1 \leq x \leq 6$.

(d) \begin{subpartsaliged}

(e) Write down the equation of the line of symmetry of the graph.

(f) The points $(-8, -97)$ and $(t, -97)$ also lie on the graph of $y = -x^2 + 5x + 7$.

Use symmetry to find the value of $t$.

\end{subpartsaliged}

(g) Write down the gradient of the line $y = 9x - 4$.

(h) Write down the equation of a line parallel to $y = -5x + 19$.

(i) 
Find the equation of line $L$ in the form $y = mx + c$.

(j) Make $x$ the subject of the formula $y = mx + c$.

(f) The points $(-8, -97)$ and $(t, -97)$ also lie on the graph of $y = -x^2 + 5x + 7$. Use symmetry to find the value of $t$. \end{subpartsaliged} (g) Write down the gradient of the line $y = 9x - 4$. (h) Write down the equation of a line parallel to $y = -5x + 19$. (i) Find the equation of line $L$ in the form $y = mx + c$. (j) Make $x$ the subject of the formula $y = mx + c$.

tex:
(a) Town $S$ is 44 km from town $R$ on a bearing of $117^\circ$.
(i) (b) Using a scale of 1 cm represents 8 km, mark the position of town $S$.

(c) Anvi cycles the 44 km from $R$ to $S$.

She leaves $R$ at 13 15 and cycles at a speed of 12 km/h.

Work out the time she arrives at $S$.

(d) A tower has a height of 16 metres.

When Jai makes a scale drawing of the tower it has a height of 20 cm.

Work out the scale Jai uses, giving your answer in the form $1 : n$.

(e) $X$, $Y$ and $Z$ are three towns.

$X$ is on a bearing of $288^\circ$ from $Y$.

$Z$ is on a bearing of $018^\circ$ from $Y$.

(i) (f) Show that angle $XYZ$ is $90^\circ$.

(g) $XY = 6$ km and $YZ = 9.7$ km.

Calculate $XZ$.

(a) $P = 3a + 5$

Find the value of $P$ when $a = 2$.

(b) Solve these equations.
(i) (c) $7x = -42$

(d) $9 (8x - 7) = 72$

(e) $5^8 \times 5^k = 5^{-24}$

Find the value of $k$.

(f) Solve the simultaneous equations.
\begin{align*}
-6x - y &= 13 \\
8x + y &= -51
\end{align*}

(g) $n$ is an integer where $n \geq -3$ and $n < 1$.

Write down all the possible values of $n$.

(h) A boy walks for 35 minutes at $x$ metres per minute.

He then runs for $t$ minutes at 160 metres per minute.

Write down an expression, in terms of $x$ and $t$, for the total distance, in metres, the boy travels.

(i) 
Find an expression for the area of this rectangle.

Give your answer in the form $x^2 + ax + b$.

(a) 120 people teach in a university mathematics department.

Some information is shown in the table.
\begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|c|}
\hline
& Lecturers & Professors & Total \\
\hline
Part-time & 11 &  &  \\
\hline
Full-time &  &  &  \\
\hline
Total &  &  & 120 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
One fifth of the people are professors.

30\% of the people are part-time.

Work out the number of full-time lecturers.

(b) $\mathscr{E} = \{\text{children in a school}\}$

$F = \{\text{children who like fruit}\}$

$V = \{\text{children who like vegetables}\}$

24 children like vegetables but do not like fruit.

8 children do not like fruit and do not like vegetables.

$n(F \cup V) = 9$ (Note: Text says $n(F+V)=9$, assuming Union or Intersection?)

$n(F) = 3 \times n(V)$

(i) (c) Complete the Venn diagram.

(d) Work out $n(F \cap V)$.
texHtml:
(a) 120 people teach in a university mathematics department. Some information is shown in the table.
LecturersProfessorsTotal
Part-time11
Full-time
Total120
One fifth of the people are professors. 30\% of the people are part-time. Work out the number of full-time lecturers. (b) $\mathscr{E} = \{\text{children in a school}\}$ $F = \{\text{children who like fruit}\}$ $V = \{\text{children who like vegetables}\}$ 24 children like vegetables but do not like fruit. 8 children do not like fruit and do not like vegetables. $n(F \cup V) = 9$ (Note: Text says $n(F+V)=9$, assuming Union or Intersection?) $n(F) = 3 \times n(V)$ (i) (c) Complete the Venn diagram. (d) Work out $n(F \cap V)$.
